description_long: This washing machine features a rectangular, upright body with a metallic gray finish and a slightly protruding control panel at the top rear. The control panel is equipped with several round buttons and a single large rotary dial, all set against a lighter-colored background for contrast. The main body is solid and boxy, with minimal external detailing except for subtle panel lines and a recessed lid on the top surface. The rear view reveals utility connections, including a large circular port and two colored hoses, as well as a vent or access panel. The machine's overall design is utilitarian, with a focus on durability and ease of use, typical of appliances found in urban laundry rooms or garages. The top-loading lid and robust construction suggest it is built for frequent, heavy-duty use.
description: A metallic gray, top-loading washing machine with a prominent rear control panel featuring buttons and a rotary dial. The design is sturdy and functional, with utility connections visible at the back.
description_view_0: Front view shows the main body, top lid, and the full control panel with buttons and a dial.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the depth of the machine and the raised control panel at the back.
description_view_2: Rear view displays utility connections, including a large circular port, two colored hoses, and a vent or access panel.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, showing the solid side panel and the profile of the control panel.
attribute_control_panel_layout: The control panel is located at the top rear of the machine, featuring four round buttons and one large rotary dial, all set on a light-colored background. The layout is linear and analog, with no visible digital display.
attribute_door_type: Top-loading rectangular lid, likely made of metal or plastic, with no visible handle or latch mechanism.
